baseline_capability_statuses.notes: Baseline now exposes governed local agent cards and capability exchange, and the compiler uses them to enrich bounded delegation posture.
Baseline now exposes a governed local extension catalog for artifact viewers and operator widgets, surfaced through runtime and GUI summaries.
Baseline now persists a governed workflow-editor state derived from planner task graphs, with review-gated editable operations and shell surfacing.
Structural graphs now extract symbol nodes from impacted files and relate them back to files/modules.
Impact summaries and retrieval hits now carry symbol/file/module context and recommended context refs.
Compiler now uses graph-grounded context packs with explicit path/symbol budgets and reduction ratios.
Structural graphs now support generic issue/PR/code-area hotspot overlays with persisted overlay artifacts and overview surfacing.
Structural graphs now emit persisted staleness reports and overview counts for missing/newer sources and age-based refresh posture.
Compiler artifacts now persist versioned task-graph seeds with explicit nodes, edges, dependencies, and replay-linked execution-graph summaries.
Planner seeds now make fan-out, fan-in, retry windows, and speculative branches explicit where the baseline policy allows them.
Planner decisions now freeze budget, cost tier, retry budget, and risk controls before worker lanes open.
Task-graph nodes now carry owner, active-slice id, collision policy, and inbox/outbox notes for bounded handoffs.
Compiler now emits explicit shapes for `single-agent`, `multi-agent-sequential`, `multi-agent-parallel`, `review-heavy`, and `safe-autofix`.
Planner seeds and execution graphs now surface approvals/checkpoints as first-class human nodes instead of implicit notes.
